name: Defense & Aerospace Sector
summary: Day 68 Wednesday. **DEFENSE DE-RATING ACCELERATES — LOCKHEED -1.8% TO $508.93, -20.2% 1M (-27% FROM MARCH HIGHS); ITA $215.37 (+0.5% 1D, -4.4% 1M).** The cleanest single-name signal that 'defense as a crisis hedge' trade has STRUCTURALLY FAILED in this cycle — AI/semiconductor rotation has been the actual crisis hedge. Decomposition: ~50% crisis-de-rating (Trump May 5 pause + diplomatic-track activation reduces defense procurement urgency); ~50% earnings (Q1 2026 EPS $6.44 vs $7.28 prior year miss; supply chain issues across fighter jet and classified programs; Q1 FCF -$291mn; multiple analyst downgrades through April). The May 4 kinetic restart did NOT bid Lockheed (defense was already structurally rotating out). The May 5 pause accelerates it. Importantly: the earnings miss is INDEPENDENT of crisis dynamics — supply chain delays will not be fixed by diplomatic-track resolution. Trump's defense-budget rhetoric (record budget passed) failed to support stock through earnings reality. Editor MUST NOT attribute 100% of LMT's -20% 1M to crisis dynamics. URA $54.20 -1.0% 1D / +11.5% 1M (uranium primarily AI-power-demand cycle, not crisis). For deployment thesis: defense rotation trade structurally failed; AI/semiconductor rotation has been the actual crisis hedge — bifurcation thesis reinforced.
